title: Workflow Builder "When person assigned" trigger
excerpt:

posts.author: pidlwick2 (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I would like an option on the workflow builder automations to be “when a person is assigned” so that an automation can run checking all boards in my workspace to see when a person is assigned and thus run an automation based off of which person is assigned, regardless of the board.

What are you trying to achieve

In this particular instance I am trying to set up an automation that will replace certain assignees with others: our area changes constantly who is over what responsibility, so we want to make it so that the position title (a team) can be assigned as the person in a people column, and then only have to update a workflow so that whenever the team is assigned it will be replaced with the current employee assigned to that position. That way when people change we only have to update one automation rather than many automations/boards.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @pidlwick2, Welcome to the community!

If it’s truly 1 role → 1 person, then the logic can work.

Here’s a workflow approach that I believe should do what you want (and keeps the mapping centralized):

  1. Instead of assigning a “team/role” in a People column, use a Dropdown column (Role/Team) to select the role.

  2. Build a workflow that triggers When Role/Team (Dropdown) changes.

  3. Add Get item data (from the current item) to capture the selected dropdown value.

  4. Add a block to Find matching items in your Resource Directory / Role Mapping board (match the dropdown value to the Role/Team name).

  5. Once the matching directory item is found, add another Get item data block to pull the People column value from that directory item (the “current employee” for that role).

  6. Add an Assign people block on your working board, using the Person value pulled from the directory board.

This way, when the person responsible for a role changes, you only update the directory board, not multiple boards/automations.

It would also be worth exploring the AI Smart Block, especially if your role naming isn’t perfectly consistent or you want smarter matching/logic.

Hope this help!

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @pidlwick2
This is a great use case. While this type of workspace-wide trigger isn’t currently available in native workflows, you can handle it using Make.

With Make, you can set up a scenario that watches multiple boards in your workspace for changes to the People column. When a person (or team/role placeholder) is assigned, Make can automatically check the value and replace it with the current employee responsible for that role.

For example:

  • Someone assigns “Marketing Lead” or a placeholder user/team.

  • Make detects that assignment.

  • It automatically replaces it with the actual person currently responsible for that position.

This way, when responsibilities change, you only need to update the mapping in one place (for example in a simple table or sheet), instead of editing automations across many boards. It keeps assignments consistent and removes a lot of maintenance work.
